name: Tajikistan

colors.symbolism: the unity of the nation, the sun's victory over darkness, and the colour of the workers
purity, the snowy peaks of the Pamirs, and the country's cotton
the fertile valleys, agriculture, and Islam
the gold of the crown and stars

description: Three horizontal stripes — red on top, a wider white in the middle, green at the bottom — in proportions 2:3:2. Centred on the white stripe, a gold crown surmounted by an arc of seven gold five-pointed stars.
history: Adopted on 24 November 1992, replacing the flag of the Tajik SSR after the country's 1991 independence. It is the only Central Asian post-Soviet national flag to retain the red of its Soviet predecessor. The seven-stars-and-crown emblem was designed to evoke Tajikistan's pre-Soviet Persian cultural heritage.

features.symbolism: red for the unity of the nation and the working class, white for purity and cotton, green for agriculture and Islam
the Tajik people — 'Tajik' is popularly etymologised as 'crowned'; also a reference to the Persian crown and the country's monarchic past

features.role: seven gold stars in an arc above the crown
features.symbolism: the number seven is sacred in Tajik tradition, signifying perfection and felicity (the 'seven heavens', the seven beautiful gardens of paradise, the seven mountains, etc.)
